DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF RUBEN TORRES' MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE FUTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND FOR AN AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 08, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302, LAW AND MOTION DEPARTMENT TO BE HEARD BY JUDGE ERNEST GOLDSMITH. JUDGE PRO TEM TO PREPARE REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION.JUDGE PRO TEM: NILS ROSENQUEST, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ALESIA COLLINS-HUDSON, CSR#7751, 415-591-3333, REPORTED. =(302/JPT)
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-08-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF RUBEN TORRES' MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE FUTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND FOR AN AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS - THE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DENIED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 32 WHICH IS VAGUE AND AMBIGUOUS. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NUMBERS 8 AND 9. THE MOTION IS GRANTED TO THE EXTENT THAT IT APPLIES ONLY TO THE BENIHANA, SAN FRANCISCO LOCATION AND ONLY PRECEDING THE PERIOD OF TIME UNTIL THE FILING OF THE COMPLAINT IN JANUARY 2014. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO THAT PERIOD OF TIME. AS TO THE PRIVACY OBJECTIONS, EMPLOYEE IDENTITY INFORMATION IS SPECIFICALLY DISCOVERABLE. SEE PUERTO V. SUPERIOR COURT (2008) 158 CAL.APP.4TH 1242; CRAB ADDISON V. SUPERIOR COURT (2008) 169 CAL.APP.4TH 958. CAL.4TH 360. THE IDENTITY INFORMATION IS DISCOVERABLE, THE INFORMATION TO TURN OVER WHICH IS THE LAST KNOWN RESIDENCE ADDRESS AND PHONE NUMBER IS SUBJECT TO THE PROTECTIVE ORDER. THE REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: TAMRA ELAINE KEEN; CSR # 5404, TEL. NO. 591-3333 (302/MJM)

Off Calendar Mar-24-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BENIHANA NATIONAL CORP.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is taken off calendar per request of the moving party. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-11-2014 Set For Trial On Jul-20-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant To Provide Futher Responses To Special Interrogatories And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-08-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PARVIZ DARABI, TEL. NO. (650) 343-5357 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. LISA LIN GARCIA OF THE LITTLER MENDELSON, TEL. NO. 288-6369 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
TAMRA ELAINE KEEN OF THE ESQUIRE DEPOSITION, TEL. NO. 591-3333, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF RUBEN TORRES' Motion To Compel Defendant To Provide Futher Responses To Special Interrogatories And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions - The motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part. Denied as to Special Interrogatory No. 32 which is vague and ambiguous. The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatories Numbers 8 and 9. The motion is granted to the extent that it applies only to the BENIHANA, SAN FRANCISCO LOCATION and only preceding the period of time until the filing of the complaint in January 2014. Parties to meet and confer as to that period of time. As to the privacy objections, employee identity information is specifically discoverable. See Puerto v. Superior Court (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 1242; Crab Addison v. Superior Court (2008) 169 Cal.App.4th 958. Cal.4th 360. The identity information is discoverable, the information to turn over which is the last known residence address and phone number is subject to the Protective order. The requests for sanctions are denied. Parties to meet and confer as to form of order. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Tamra Elaine Keen; CSR # 5404, Tel. No. 591-3333 =302/mjm

Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant To Provide Futher Responses To Special Interrogatories And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-25-2014 Continued To Aug-08-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Parviz Darabi Esq(650-343-5357) for Ruben Torres Plaintiff and moving party
Lisa Lin Garcia Esq of Littler (415-733-1940) for Benihana National Corp., Defendant and opposing party
Court Reporter: Alesia Collins-Hudson, CSR#7751, 415-591-3333, Reported.

Defendant's Counsel does not stipulate to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:
PLAINTIFF RUBEN TORRES' Motion To Compel Defendant To Provide Futher Responses To Special Interrogatories And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions is continued to August 08, 2014 at 9:30 am in Department 302, Law and Motion Department to be heard by Judge Ernest Goldsmith.
Judge Pro tem to prepare report and recommendation.Judge Pro Tem: Nils Rosenquest, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Alesia Collins-Hudson, CSR#7751, 415-591-3333, Reported. =(302/JPT)
